description: Two birds, acting as a mated pair. Size identical. Shape identical except one (male) had a larger head. The description of the male is as follows: A small goose not much larger than Mallards, with black legs and feet, very small black bill, black head and neck (except for bright white crescent-shaped mark on each side of neck), well-defined black breast. Lower breast and mid-belly a mixture of gray-brown to off-white. Sides pale gray broadly marked whitish. Lower belly and vent bright white. White tail with small black corners barely noticeable Cold gray-brown upperparts with blackish flight feathers folded neatly above the rump. The female plumage was identical to the male except as noted below: the white mark on teh side of the neck not as bright. The whitish wash to the sides less obvious, giving the underparts a slightly darker look. Border of the black upper breast less sharp.Behaviors: Eating grass on shore with American Wigeons and Mallards. When flushed off the shoreline into the water, the pair floated among a large group of Richardson's Cackling Geese.Call: nonePlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar
